title: STANDARD FOR A BLEND OF SWEETENED

sections.section: STANDARD FOR A BLEND OF SWEETENED
sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for a blend of sweetened (not substantive regulatory text).

sections.section: CONDENSED SKIMMED MILK AND VEGETABLE FAT CXS 252-2006
sections.summary: Document title with standard identifier: Condensed Skimmed Milk and Vegetable Fat CXS 252-2006 (title only).
States adoption and amendment history: adopted 2006 and amended in 2010, 2018, 2021 and 2022.
Amendment heading only (2022 Amendment).

sections.section: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty-fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
sections.summary: Records a Codex amendment from December 2022 that amends Section 7.6 to require labelling of non‑retail containers in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).
Scope: applies to blends of sweetened condensed skimmed milk and vegetable fat intended for direct consumption or further processing, per the product description in Section 2.
Defines the product as a blend made by recombining milk constituents or partially removing water, with added sugar and edible vegetable oil/fat to meet the compositional requirements in Section 3.

Specifies permitted raw materials (skimmed milk, skimmed milk powders, other non‑fat milk solids, edible vegetable fats/oils), allowed protein adjustment products (milk retentate, milk permeate, lactose), permitted ingredients (potable water, sugar, NaCl/KCl), nutrient addition rules per CXG 9‑1987, and compositional minima: standard product minimum total fat 8% m/m, milk solids‑not‑fat 20% m/m, milk protein in MSNF 34% m/m, with a reduced‑fat variant >1% and <8% fat; sugar levels are limited by Good Manufacturing Practice to prevent crystallization.

Lists allowed raw materials (skimmed milk and powders, other non‑fat milk solids, edible vegetable fats/oils), permits milk retentate/permeate and lactose for protein adjustment, lists potable water, sugar and salt substitutes as permitted ingredients, references nutrient addition rules, sets compositional minima and reduced‑fat range (as in Section 3.4), and notes sugar limits by GMP.

Permits only technologically justified additive classes for this product, explicitly allowing certain acidity regulators (per CXS 192‑1995 for beverage whiteners) and specified emulsifiers, stabilizers and thickeners, while disallowing colours, bleaching agents, antioxidants, preservatives, foaming agents, anticaking agents and packaging gases.

Requires that the product and its milk and vegetable oil/fat ingredients comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with CAC maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57‑2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and compliance with microbiological criteria per CXG 21‑1997.

Adds specific labelling provisions to the general pre‑packaged foods standard: prescribed product names, declaration formats for total fat and milk protein, statement of presence/source of vegetable fat/oil when required, ingredient exemption for milk used only for protein adjustment, mandatory advisory that product is not a substitute for infant formula, and non‑retail container labelling per CXS 346‑2021.

Requires the name to be either 'Blend of Sweetened Condensed Skimmed Milk and Vegetable Fat' or 'Reduced Fat Blend of Sweetened Condensed Skimmed Milk and Vegetable Fat', with other names allowed if national legislation permits.

Requires declaration of total fat either as a percentage by mass/volume or grams per serving, and mandates a label statement of the presence of edible vegetable fat/oil and, where required by the country of sale, the common vegetable source.
Requires declaration of milk protein content either as a percentage by mass/volume or in grams per serving, in a manner acceptable in the country of sale.
Allows omission from the ingredients list of milk products used solely for protein adjustment despite the general pre‑packaged foods labelling provision.
Mandates an advisory statement on the label that the product should not be used as a substitute for infant formula (e.g., 'NOT SUITABLE FOR INFANTS').
Requires labelling of non‑retail containers to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Requires that, for checking compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the Standard's provisions shall be used.
